outputs.output:
- `intake_summary`: Insufficient to expand planning. This brief matches the repo smoke scenario, but that path is not execution-deterministic until the provider is explicit: the sub-agent allows only `provider-codex` or `provider-claude`, so keep work in the `front_door_analysis` parallel wave and block any handoff into `blueprint-planner` or later sequential stages.
- `clarification_questions`: Which allowed provider should replace the default scenario routing for this run: `provider-codex` or `provider-claude`? If the intent is cross-host replay, confirm whether the host should emit dual payloads for both instead of one resolved provider.
- `acceptance_candidates`: Use only repo-defined contracts, preserve topology-owned parallel versus sequential routing, and emit self-contained prompt packets plus provider-ready payloads with the downstream artifacts `subagent_execution_plan.json`, `subagent_prompt_packets.md`, and `subagent_execution_result.json` so another host can execute without hidden chat memory.

- `work_type_decision`: `new_feature`. The scenario matches `.ghdp/orchestrate/scenarios/new_feature_subagent_smoke.json`, which is explicitly about proving a net-new deterministic sub-agent/provider execution flow; `enhancement` or `maintenance` would under-scope that lifecycle.
- Topology routing: run `work-type-classifier` in the `front_door_analysis` parallel wave from `.ghdp/orchestrate/topology.json` with `ticket-intake`, `autonomy-assessor`, `context-capability-discovery`, and `parallel-work-awareness`, and complete this artifact before any sequential `delivery_backbone` stage consumes it.
- Provider-ready payload: emit a self-contained `work_type_decision` packet for `provider-codex` or `provider-claude` that includes `work_type=new_feature`, the routing rationale, the scenario brief, the prompt contract, and the allowed skill/plugin lists so another host can execute it without hidden chat memory.

- `autonomy_decision`: `bounded-medium`. Ambiguity is low to moderate because the repo-level agent, skill, topology, and provider contracts are explicit; blast radius is moderate because the output steers downstream execution; reversibility is high because this stage produces stateless packet artifacts, not direct implementation changes.
- `gating_rationale`: keep this sub-agent scoped to `stage4_autonomy` in the `front_door_analysis` parallel wave and emit only self-contained `autonomy_decision` and `gating_rationale` content that carries the scenario brief, allowed skill `complexity-autonomy-assessment`, and allowed plugins `provider-codex` and `provider-claude` so another host can execute without hidden chat memory.
- Escalate and reduce autonomy to `low` if the task expands into host/plugin resolution beyond `provider-codex` or `provider-claude`, if parallel vs sequential ownership is not fully determined by `.ghdp/orchestrate/topology.json`, or if the packet would depend on unstated repo context; those are kernel/orchestrator concerns, not this sub-agent’s safe execution boundary.

- `capability_map`: Reuse the existing repo-contract pipeline before adding anything new: [context-capability-discovery.json](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/agents/context-capability-discovery.json:1) already bounds this agent, [orchestrate_front_door.py](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/platform-cli/src/platform_cli/tools/orchestrate_front_door.py:401) already derives `capability_matches` and `impacted_areas` from `.ghdp/agents`, `.ghdp/skills`, and `.ghdp/plugins`, and [subagents.py](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/platform-cli/src/platform_cli/orchestrate_kernel/subagents.py:142) already persists prompt-packet artifacts for host execution.

- `impacted_areas`: Keep this in the topology’s `front_door_analysis` wave from [topology.json](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/topology.json:5), then hand off through the existing scenario/runtime path in [new_feature_subagent_smoke.json](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/scenarios/new_feature_subagent_smoke.json:1) and [orchestrate.py](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/platform-cli/src/platform_cli/commands/orchestrate.py:445); grounded touched surfaces are `.ghdp/agents`, `.ghdp/skills`, `.ghdp/plugins`, `.ghdp/orchestrate/scenarios`, `platform-cli/src/platform_cli/tools/orchestrate_front_door.py`, and `platform-cli/src/platform_cli/orchestrate_kernel/*`.

- `sync_decision`: Stay repo-native by default: write `subagent_execution_plan.json`, `subagent_prompt_packets.md`, and `subagent_execution_result.json` under `.ghdp/orchestrate` per [orchestrate/README.md](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/README.md:3) and [memory/README.md](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/memory/README.md:21), route provider-ready payloads through `provider-codex` or `provider-claude` using [provider_adapters.py](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/platform-cli/src/platform_cli/orchestrate_kernel/provider_adapters.py:29), and only invoke `sync-minimal` if ownership clearly crosses into managed `~/.ghdp` policy or state.

- `parallel_work_decision: block_and_resume`. Per `.ghdp/orchestrate/topology.json`, `parallel-work-awareness` stays in the `front_door_analysis` parallel wave only; do not hand this scenario into the sequential `delivery_backbone` until the overlap signal is resolved.

- `Conflict signal: explicit.` The current branch already has an active run: [branch_state.json](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/branches/feature-eppe-7391-technical-agentic-framework-foundation-skills-plugins-subagents-native-memory/branch_state.json>) is `paused` with `active_run_key=20260504-223548-ist__codex__stage-a` and next action `Stage 22 traceability capture`, while [run_state.json](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/branches/feature-eppe-7391-technical-agentic-framework-foundation-skills-plugins-subagents-native-memory/runs/20260504-223548-ist__codex__stage-a/run_state.json>) is still `in_progress`; treat a new start as duplicate work and resume the existing run instead.

- `Provider-ready payload:` build the next-host packet for `provider-codex` or `provider-claude` from repo-backed artifacts only: `.ghdp/agents/parallel-work-awareness.json`, `.ghdp/skills/folder-backed-shared-memory/SKILL.md`, `.ghdp/skills/traceability-and-resume/SKILL.md`, [handoff.md](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/branches/feature-eppe-7391-technical-agentic-framework-foundation-skills-plugins-subagents-native-memory/handoff.md>), and [resume_context.md](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/branches/feature-eppe-7391-technical-agentic-framework-foundation-skills-plugins-subagents-native-memory/runs/20260504-223548-ist__codex__stage-a/resume_context.md>); set `conflict_status=resume_existing_run`. `.ghdp/memory/shared` has no durable overlap record and `~/.ghdp` shows no stronger lock/heartbeat, so prefer this repo-local conflict signal over silent assumption.

- Run the topology in contract order: complete the `front_door_analysis` wave in parallel, execute `blueprint-planner` in the sequential backbone, then hand off to the review wave; do not flatten or reorder work outside [.ghdp/orchestrate/topology.json](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/topology.json:1).

- Use `blueprint-poa-authoring` to update one canonical [poa.md](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/branches/feature-eppe-7391-technical-agentic-framework-foundation-skills-plugins-subagents-native-memory/poa.md:1) from [.ghdp/frbr/intent.json](/C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/frbr/intent.json:1) plus the front-door outputs, carrying forward intake, capability discovery, impacted areas, and the exact downstream execution basis.

- Use `stable-release-notes-assembly` to record concise release implications in that same POA and emit self-contained prompt packets/provider payloads for `provider-codex` and `provider-claude`, so another host can execute the planner stage without hidden chat memory.

- `BLOCK`: scheduling is still partly scenario-owned, not topology-owned. [subagents.py](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/platform-cli/src/platform_cli/orchestrate_kernel/subagents.py:261>) builds waves by iterating `requested_agents`, while [topology.json](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/topology.json:5>) and [kernel.json](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/kernel.json:4>) say execution is repo-contract-driven. Make topology the only source of wave order; let scenarios select subsets only.

- `ACCEPT`: per-agent provider policy is now enforced at the runtime boundary. [subagents.py](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/platform-cli/src/platform_cli/orchestrate_kernel/subagents.py:218>) rejects an `effective_plugin` outside each agent’s `allowed_plugins`, and both [architecture-review.json](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/agents/architecture-review.json:8>) and [ux-dx-review.json](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/agents/ux-dx-review.json:8>) are compatible with headless Codex/Claude execution. Keep this strict; do not move provider selection back into host convention.

- `BLOCK`: “repo-defined contracts only” is still diluted by runtime/headless gaps. [subagents.py](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/platform-cli/src/platform_cli/orchestrate_kernel/subagents.py:227>) hardcodes prompt scaffolding and the 3-bullet output rule, [provider-codex/plugin.json](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/plugins/provider-codex/plugin.json:7>) and [provider-claude/plugin.json](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/plugins/provider-claude/plugin.json:6>) omit topology that the VS Code adapters carry, and [subagents.py](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/platform-cli/src/platform_cli/orchestrate_kernel/subagents.py:172>) only publishes artifact names. Move prompt template, topology obligations, and artifact path/schema into `.ghdp` contracts before calling this host-independent.

- `Block:` The headless provider payloads are not topology-complete. [provider-codex](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/plugins/provider-codex/plugin.json:1>) and [provider-claude](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/plugins/provider-claude/plugin.json:1>) omit `.ghdp/orchestrate/topology.json`, while the topology contract is the source of parallel vs sequential scheduling in [topology.json](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/orchestrate/topology.json:5>). Mirror the richer VS Code provider contracts so another host does not need out-of-band orchestration knowledge.

- `Block:` Execution order is still partly hidden in scenario ordering instead of being fully derived from the topology contract. [plan_execution_waves()](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/platform-cli/src/platform_cli/orchestrate_kernel/subagents.py:261>) walks `requested_agents` and groups opportunistically, so a host must trust scenario order rather than the canonical contract. Make the persisted plan topology-and-dependency derived first, then replay from that saved schedule only.

- `Gap:` The prompt packets are readable but not fully executable as handoff artifacts. [ux-dx-review.json](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/.ghdp/agents/ux-dx-review.json:9>) declares only `ux_dx_findings`, and [persist_scenario_result()](</C:/Users/Hi/Downloads/git-repos/dp-tools-local-setup/platform-cli/src/platform_cli/orchestrate_kernel/subagents.py:159>) writes artifact names without required path, format, or schema. Add explicit output location and minimal payload shape to `subagent_execution_plan.json` so the next host can write the artifact without repo intuition.

You are the repo-defined sub-agent 'ticket-intake'.
Role: input_clarifier
Use only the allowed skills and plugins below.
Return a short execution-ready assessment for this scenario in 3 bullets.

Scenario brief:
- Use repo-defined agent contracts only.
- Respect the topology contract for parallel versus sequential work.
- Produce prompt packets and provider-ready payloads that another host can execute without hidden chat memory.

Prompt contract:
- Decide whether intake is sufficient before planning expands.
- Prefer precise clarification requests over vague open-ended follow-up.
- Carry acceptance criteria forward when they are present.
